Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭= Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} (spring) / {{ill|lt=September 16|Q2897|WD=Q2897|1=September 16}} (autumn) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawase Shrine''' (かわせじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hikone|Q386635|WD=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is treated as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Kawaketa Shrine" of Kanzaki District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (a candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}})As candidate shrines for "Kawaketa Shrine," six are known: {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine|Q11553975|WD=Q11553975|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine}} (Kanda-chō, Yōkaichi, now {{ill|lt=Higashiōmi|Q240891|WD=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Obata Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Gokashō Nakamachi, Higashiōmi), {{ill|lt=Ōtaki Shrine (Higashiōmi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtaki Shrine}} (Kayao-chō, Higashiōmi), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawawaki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Nakajuku, {{ill|lt=Aishō|Q1346806|WD=Q1346806|1=Aishō, Shiga}}, Aichi District), {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Shrine (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawaketa Shrine}} (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone), and this shrine in the former Inukami District.. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|lt=gōsha|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are three {{ill|lt=pillars|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. If this shrine was the Engishiki "Kawaketa Shrine," many theories hold that the original deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yukawaketa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}For example: {{ill|lt=Deguchi Nobutsune|Q108780926|WD=Q108780926|1=Deguchi Nobutsune}}, '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}'; {{ill|lt=Kurita Hiroshi|Q6536170|WD=Q6536170|1=Kurita Hiroshi}}, '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingishi-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}}'; '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'; and '{{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Shi|Q1156003|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}'. '{{ill|lt=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|WD=Q85883966|1=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku}}' identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha but does not state the enshrined deity, and '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}' says "deity unknown.". It has long been worshiped as a deity of commerce. Because "Kawase" can also be read as "exchange," it also attracts worship from people in finance. === History === The founding date is unknown, but until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Kawaketa Shrine," "Keta-no-miya," and "Keta Daimyōjin."{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.hikone.shiga.jp/kikakushinkobu/johoseisaku/pdf/20120501KH08.pdf Kōhō Hikone, May 1 2012 issue, p.17, serial feature: My town in the Sengoku era, No.22 - Kawase Castle - flatland fort linked to the Kawase clan]}} In {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), the lords of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawase-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://kids.city.hikone.shiga.jp/carta/card_so.html Hikone City Hall: Learn Hikone history through karuta], the Kawase clan, repaired the shrine buildings and donated rice fields. Through the Edo period it was revered by the Ii lords of {{ill|lt=Hikone Domain|Q1062225|WD=Q1062225|1=Hikone Domain}}, and at major festivals a {{ill|lt=magistrate|Q2535820|WD=Q2535820|1=Bugyō}} was dispatched for ceremonial security'Shintō Daijiten.'. According to "Jinja Yūshoki" compiled by Shiga Prefecture's education section, in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|WD=Q7624|1=1675}}), Kawase Iki-no-kami and his grandson Kawase Hayato-no-shō, former local lords of Kawase-no-shō, re-established the shrine by inviting {{ill|lt=aiden deities|Q135009883|WD=Q135009883|lt=aiden deities|1=Aidono}}Which deity/deities these aiden referred to is unknown.. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era it was renamed "Kawase Shrine." It was ranked gōsha on January 17, Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), temporarily demoted to {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} on October 21, Meiji 9, restored to gōsha again on February 1, Meiji 14, and designated as a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku offering shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}} on April 29, Meiji 41. A 2012 survey by Hikone City's cultural-properties section estimated nearby remains to be those of {{ill|lt=Kawase Castle|Q11554012|WD=Q11554012|1=Kawase Castle}}. === Shrine buildings === The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is a {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure. It was relocated in {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) from shrine buildings of {{ill|lt=Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. There are also an {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} and a {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}. ファイル:Kawase jinja honden enkei.JPG|'''Distant view of honden''' ファイル:Kawase jinja honden syoumen.JPG|'''Front of honden''' === Precinct shrines === Isorowa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Wakamatsu Shrine, Hachidai Shrine, Tennei Shrine, Kaminoki Shrine, Tsujinaka Shrine, Tenmangū-sha, Tenman Shrine === Precinct === * Monument to Tanemura Genpei (Horsehead commemorative stele) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kawase Station|Q614782|WD=Q614782|1=Kawase Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}} ({{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}), take the reservation-only Ainori Taxi Kawase to Kawasebabachō Kōminkan stop; 2 minutes on foot. * About 12 minutes on foot from Kawase Station. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (ed.), 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Tōsandō 1,' {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981 * Supervised by {{ill|lt=Miyaji Naoichi|Q11453535|WD=Q11453535|1=Naokazu Miyaji}} and {{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q11382847|WD=Q11382847|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}}, 'Shintō Daijiten (compact edition),' {{ill|lt=Rinsen Shoten|Q11612454|WD=Q11612454|1=Rinsen Shoten}}, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (first edition by {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, 1937) === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=182 Kawase Shrine (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250232/ Kawase Shrine (Yaokami)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Hikone, Shiga]]

{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭= Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|WD=Q2519|1=April 16}} (spring) / {{ill|lt=September 16|Q2897|WD=Q2897|1=September 16}} (autumn) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawase Shrine''' (かわせじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hikone|Q386635|WD=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is treated as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Kawaketa Shrine" of Kanzaki District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|WD=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (a candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}})As candidate shrines for "Kawaketa Shrine," six are known: {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine|Q11553975|WD=Q11553975|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine}} (Kanda-chō, Yōkaichi, now {{ill|lt=Higashiōmi|Q240891|WD=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Obata Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Gokashō Nakamachi, Higashiōmi), {{ill|lt=Ōtaki Shrine (Higashiōmi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtaki Shrine}} (Kayao-chō, Higashiōmi), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawawaki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Nakajuku, {{ill|lt=Aishō|Q1346806|WD=Q1346806|1=Aishō, Shiga}}, Aichi District), {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Shrine (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawaketa Shrine}} (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone), and this shrine in the former Inukami District.. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|lt=gōsha|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are three {{ill|lt=pillars|Q4817|WD=Q4817|1=Column}}: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. If this shrine was the Engishiki "Kawaketa Shrine," many theories hold that the original deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yukawaketa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}For example: {{ill|lt=Deguchi Nobutsune|Q108780926|WD=Q108780926|1=Deguchi Nobutsune}}, '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|WD=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}'; {{ill|lt=Kurita Hiroshi|Q6536170|WD=Q6536170|1=Kurita Hiroshi}}, '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingishi-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}}'; '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|WD=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'; and '{{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Shi|Q1156003|WD=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}'. '{{ill|lt=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|WD=Q85883966|1=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku}}' identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha but does not state the enshrined deity, and '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|WD=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}' says "deity unknown.". It has long been worshiped as a deity of commerce. Because "Kawase" can also be read as "exchange," it also attracts worship from people in finance. === History === The founding date is unknown, but until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Kawaketa Shrine," "Keta-no-miya," and "Keta Daimyōjin."{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.hikone.shiga.jp/kikakushinkobu/johoseisaku/pdf/20120501KH08.pdf Kōhō Hikone, May 1 2012 issue, p.17, serial feature: My town in the Sengoku era, No.22 - Kawase Castle - flatland fort linked to the Kawase clan]}} In {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|WD=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), the lords of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawase-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://kids.city.hikone.shiga.jp/carta/card_so.html Hikone City Hall: Learn Hikone history through karuta], the Kawase clan, repaired the shrine buildings and donated rice fields. Through the Edo period it was revered by the Ii lords of {{ill|lt=Hikone Domain|Q1062225|WD=Q1062225|1=Hikone Domain}}, and at major festivals a {{ill|lt=magistrate|Q2535820|WD=Q2535820|1=Bugyō}} was dispatched for ceremonial security'Shintō Daijiten.'. According to "Jinja Yūshoki" compiled by Shiga Prefecture's education section, in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|WD=Q7624|1=1675}}), Kawase Iki-no-kami and his grandson Kawase Hayato-no-shō, former local lords of Kawase-no-shō, re-established the shrine by inviting {{ill|lt=aiden deities|Q135009883|WD=Q135009883|lt=aiden deities|1=Aidono}}Which deity/deities these aiden referred to is unknown.. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era it was renamed "Kawase Shrine." It was ranked gōsha on January 17, Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), temporarily demoted to {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} on October 21, Meiji 9, restored to gōsha again on February 1, Meiji 14, and designated as a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku offering shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}} on April 29, Meiji 41. A 2012 survey by Hikone City's cultural-properties section estimated nearby remains to be those of {{ill|lt=Kawase Castle|Q11554012|WD=Q11554012|1=Kawase Castle}}. === Shrine buildings === The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|WD=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is a {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure. It was relocated in {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|WD=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|WD=Q7704|1=1864}}) from shrine buildings of {{ill|lt=Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|WD=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|WD=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. There are also an {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|WD=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} and a {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|WD=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}. ファイル:Kawase jinja honden enkei.JPG|'''Distant view of honden''' ファイル:Kawase jinja honden syoumen.JPG|'''Front of honden''' === Precinct shrines === Isorowa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Wakamatsu Shrine, Hachidai Shrine, Tennei Shrine, Kaminoki Shrine, Tsujinaka Shrine, Tenmangū-sha, Tenman Shrine === Precinct === * Monument to Tanemura Genpei (Horsehead commemorative stele) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kawase Station|Q614782|WD=Q614782|1=Kawase Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|WD=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}} ({{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}), take the reservation-only Ainori Taxi Kawase to Kawasebabachō Kōminkan stop; 2 minutes on foot. * About 12 minutes on foot from Kawase Station. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (ed.), 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Tōsandō 1,' {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981 * Supervised by {{ill|lt=Miyaji Naoichi|Q11453535|WD=Q11453535|1=Naokazu Miyaji}} and {{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q11382847|WD=Q11382847|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}}, 'Shintō Daijiten (compact edition),' {{ill|lt=Rinsen Shoten|Q11612454|WD=Q11612454|1=Rinsen Shoten}}, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (first edition by {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, 1937) === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=182 Kawase Shrine (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250232/ Kawase Shrine (Yaokami)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone, Shiga]]

{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small)
Former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭= Sunday nearest {{ill|lt=April 16|Q2519|qid=Q2519|1=April 16}} (spring) / {{ill|lt=September 16|Q2897|qid=Q2897|1=September 16}} (autumn) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kawase Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kawase Shrine''' (かわせじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Hikone|Q386635|qid=Q386635|1=Hikone, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is treated as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for "Kawaketa Shrine" of Kanzaki District, {{ill|lt=Ōmi Province|Q843882|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}}, listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' (a candidate {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}})As candidate shrines for "Kawaketa Shrine," six are known: {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Mikabe Shrine|Q11553975|qid=Q11553975|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine}} (Kanda-chō, Yōkaichi, now {{ill|lt=Higashiōmi|Q240891|qid=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Obata Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Gokashō Nakamachi, Higashiōmi), {{ill|lt=Ōtaki Shrine (Higashiōmi)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtaki Shrine}} (Kayao-chō, Higashiōmi), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawawaki Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} (Nakajuku, {{ill|lt=Aishō|Q1346806|qid=Q1346806|1=Aishō, Shiga}}, Aichi District), {{ill|lt=Kawaketa Shrine (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone)|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawaketa Shrine}} (Kōsaki-chō, Hikone), and this shrine in the former Inukami District.. It was formerly ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|lt=gōsha|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The current {{ill|lt=enshrined deities|Q11591100|qid=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} are three {{ill|lt=pillars|Q4817|qid=Q4817|1=Column}}: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. If this shrine was the Engishiki "Kawaketa Shrine," many theories hold that the original deity was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Yukawaketa-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}For example: {{ill|lt=Deguchi Nobutsune|Q108780926|qid=Q108780926|1=Deguchi Nobutsune}}, '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō Kōshō|Q11588824|qid=Q11588824|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō Studies}}'; {{ill|lt=Kurita Hiroshi|Q6536170|qid=Q6536170|1=Kurita Hiroshi}}, '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Jingishi-ryō|1=UNKNOWN}}'; '{{ill|lt=Tokusen Jinmyōchō|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'; and '{{ill|lt=Dai Nihon Shi|Q1156003|qid=Q1156003|lt=Dai Nihon Shi Jingi-shi|1=Dai Nihonshi}}'. '{{ill|lt=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku|Q85883966|qid=Q85883966|1=Ōmi Yochi Shiryaku}}' identifies this shrine as the Shikinaisha but does not state the enshrined deity, and '{{ill|lt=Jinja Kakuroku|Q11590708|qid=Q11590708|1=Jinja Kakuroku}}' says "deity unknown.". It has long been worshiped as a deity of commerce. Because "Kawase" can also be read as "exchange," it also attracts worship from people in finance. === History === The founding date is unknown, but until the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was called "Kawaketa Shrine," "Keta-no-miya," and "Keta Daimyōjin."{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.hikone.shiga.jp/kikakushinkobu/johoseisaku/pdf/20120501KH08.pdf Kōhō Hikone, May 1 2012 issue, p.17, serial feature: My town in the Sengoku era, No.22 - Kawase Castle - flatland fort linked to the Kawase clan]}} In {{ill|lt=Genki|Q1145064|qid=Q1145064|1=Genki (era)}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|qid=Q6659|1=1570}}), the lords of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kawase-no-shō|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://kids.city.hikone.shiga.jp/carta/card_so.html Hikone City Hall: Learn Hikone history through karuta], the Kawase clan, repaired the shrine buildings and donated rice fields. Through the Edo period it was revered by the Ii lords of {{ill|lt=Hikone Domain|Q1062225|qid=Q1062225|1=Hikone Domain}}, and at major festivals a {{ill|lt=magistrate|Q2535820|qid=Q2535820|1=Bugyō}} was dispatched for ceremonial security'Shintō Daijiten.'. According to "Jinja Yūshoki" compiled by Shiga Prefecture's education section, in {{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1675|Q7624|qid=Q7624|1=1675}}), Kawase Iki-no-kami and his grandson Kawase Hayato-no-shō, former local lords of Kawase-no-shō, re-established the shrine by inviting {{ill|lt=aiden deities|Q135009883|qid=Q135009883|lt=aiden deities|1=Aidono}}Which deity/deities these aiden referred to is unknown.. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era it was renamed "Kawase Shrine." It was ranked gōsha on January 17, Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), temporarily demoted to {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} on October 21, Meiji 9, restored to gōsha again on February 1, Meiji 14, and designated as a {{ill|lt=shinsen-heihaku offering shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and cloth}} on April 29, Meiji 41. A 2012 survey by Hikone City's cultural-properties section estimated nearby remains to be those of {{ill|lt=Kawase Castle|Q11554012|qid=Q11554012|1=Kawase Castle}}. === Shrine buildings === The {{ill|lt=honden|Q2044994|qid=Q2044994|1=Honden}} is a {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=sangen-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure. It was relocated in {{ill|lt=Genji|Q1325419|qid=Q1325419|1=Genji era}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1864|Q7704|qid=Q7704|1=1864}}) from shrine buildings of {{ill|lt=Kamowakeikazuchi Shrine|Q700448|qid=Q700448|1=Kamigamo Shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kyoto|Q34600|qid=Q34600|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|qid=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. There are also an {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} {{ill|lt=haiden|Q3125745|qid=Q3125745|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} and a {{ill|lt=kaguraden|Q94498|qid=Q94498|1=Kagura-den}}. ファイル:Kawase jinja honden enkei.JPG|'''Distant view of honden''' ファイル:Kawase jinja honden syoumen.JPG|'''Front of honden''' === Precinct shrines === Isorowa Shrine, Hachiman Shrine, Wakamatsu Shrine, Hachidai Shrine, Tennei Shrine, Kaminoki Shrine, Tsujinaka Shrine, Tenmangū-sha, Tenman Shrine === Precinct === * Monument to Tanemura Genpei (Horsehead commemorative stele) === Access === * From {{ill|lt=Kawase Station|Q614782|qid=Q614782|1=Kawase Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Biwako Line|Q804135|qid=Q804135|1=Biwako Line}} ({{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|qid=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}), take the reservation-only Ainori Taxi Kawase to Kawasebabachō Kōminkan stop; 2 minutes on foot. * About 12 minutes on foot from Kawase Station. === Notes === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (ed.), 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku Tōsandō 1,' {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kōgakkan University Press|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981 * Supervised by {{ill|lt=Miyaji Naoichi|Q11453535|qid=Q11453535|1=Naokazu Miyaji}} and {{ill|lt=Saeki Ariyoshi|Q11382847|qid=Q11382847|1=Saeki Ariyoshi}}, 'Shintō Daijiten (compact edition),' {{ill|lt=Rinsen Shoten|Q11612454|qid=Q11612454|1=Rinsen Shoten}}, 1969, ISBN 4-653-01347-0 (first edition by {{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}, 1937) === External links === * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=182 Kawase Shrine (Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office)] * [https://yaokami.jp/1250232/ Kawase Shrine (Yaokami)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kawase Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:彦根市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hikone, Shiga]]
